_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX House San Antonio/Bexar delegation HD119-123: Campos (D), Gervin-Hawkins (D), LaHood (R), Dorazio (R), Bernal (D). Roll-call + issue-group + endorsement evidence, each cited. Every TRUE/FALSE has a source; unsupported cells null via reset_unspecified. No cross-party deviations found among the three Democrats; LaHood carries a fiscal-moderate flag (TX Scorecard Index 74).

profile.background: San Antonio Democrat, HD-119 (Bexar Co.), in office since Jan 2021; ~30-yr legal administrator, former Capitol staffer. Public Health Vice Chair (89th). Running in the March 2026 Democratic primary.

elizabeth-campos@TX.notes_set: Evidence pass 2026-06-19. Conventional San Antonio Democrat; NO genuine cross-party deviation on high-value cells. Voted NO on SB8 heartbeat (2021), NO on HB1927 permitless carry (2021), NO on SB2 vouchers (2025). Called Operation Lone Star wasteful. Corrected a false third-party signal: the Freedom Index wrongly flagged her YES on SB8/HB1927 (contradicted by primary roll calls). Cautious documented credit on public-safety: publicly backs officers + authored peace-officer mental-health bills (HB1593/HB3420). Religion undocumented.

1.note: Voted NO on SB8 (2021 Heartbeat Act) final House passage; only Guillen crossed over as lone D YES.

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 (2025) ESA/voucher; publicly refused Abbott's trade of her dog bill for a voucher vote.
Documented credit: publicly backs law enforcement ('officers put their lives on the line') and authored HB1593/HB3420 mental-health support for peace officers/firefighters.
Calls Operation Lone Star (the active state border/military operation) wasteful, ~$9,000/person with no tangible results.
Voted NO on HB1927 (2021 permitless/constitutional carry); the 7 D crossover YES voters are named and she is not among them.

profile.background: East-side San Antonio Democrat, HD-120 (Bexar Co.), in TX House since 2017. Sister of NBA HOF George Gervin; founder of George Gervin Academy charter school. Won the March 2026 Democratic primary, unopposed in the general. Committees: Appropriations, Redistricting, Ways & Means.

barbara-gervin-hawkins@TX.notes_set: Evidence pass 2026-06-19. Straight-line progressive Democrat; ZERO cross-party deviations. School-choice deviation checked closely (she runs a charter school) and DEBUNKED: voted to strip the ESA voucher provision in 2023 and NO on SB2 vouchers in 2025. Texas Values Action Faith & Family 2025 = 0% (itemized contrary votes); Texas Freedom Index 16% cumulative; endorsed by Annie's List (pro-choice PAC). Religion undocumented. Note: prior file had a mis-sourced Tennessee State Rifle Association footnote (wrong state) — superseded by this pass.

1.note: Voted AGAINST SB8 / Women's Privacy Act (both readings) per Texas Values Action 2025 (0% rating).

4.note: Endorsed/backed by Annie's List, a pro-choice candidate-funding PAC.

2.note: Voted AGAINST HB229 'What is a Woman Act' and SB1257 'Detransitioners Bill of Rights' (2025).

4.note: Consistent pro-LGBTQ voting record on the 2025 social slate (Texas Values Action 0%).

0.note: Voted to strip the ESA voucher provision (2023) and NO on SB2 school choice (2025), despite running a charter school.

1.note: Voted AGAINST SB12 Parents' Bill of Rights (2025).

2.note: Voted AGAINST SB13 (removing harmful/explicit library materials) (2025).

3.note: Voted AGAINST SB10 (Ten Commandments in schools) and SB11 (prayer in schools) (2025).

0.note: HB1927 (2021 permitless carry) passed on party lines; the named D crossover YES voters do not include her, placing her on the NO side.

profile.background: Freshman San Antonio Republican, HD-121 (Bexar Co.); criminal-defense attorney (brother of former Bexar DA Nico LaHood). Won the 2024 GOP primary defeating anti-voucher incumbent Steve Allison (Abbott-backed), then the general; won the March 2026 GOP primary, faces a Democrat in November 2026. Committees: Judiciary & Civil Jurisprudence, Transportation.

marc-lahood@TX.notes_set: Evidence pass 2026-06-19. Solid conservative freshman on life, sexuality, school choice (marquee SB2 YES), and Christian liberty (SB10). Texas Values Action 2025 = 86%; Texas Right to Life PAC endorsed; Trump/Abbott/Cruz endorsements. FISCAL-MODERATE FLAG: Texans for Fiscal Responsibility 2025 Index = 74 (House avg 58; champion grade 90+) — above average but not a fiscal champion. One off-position org mark: voted for HB1738 (repeal of the dead-letter, post-Lawrence sodomy statute, alongside conservative co-sponsors Harrison/Phelan) — context-dependent, not scored as a clean deviation. Thin first-session record: many cells null rather than inferred. Faith: campaign affirms active Christian faith; specific denomination undocumented.

0.note: Campaigns on a 'culture of life'; Texas Right to Life PAC endorsed him as a 'steadfast defender of innocent human life'; supported SB8, SB33 (no taxpayer abortion funding).

2.note: Supported HB229 'What is a Woman Act' and SB1257 Detransitioners Bill of Rights (2025).

4.note: Backed the 2025 sex/gender legislative slate; campaign opposes 'men competing in girls' sport.'

0.note: Voted YES on SB2 (2025 ESA/voucher); his 2024 win was powered by Abbott's pro-voucher endorsement vs anti-voucher incumbent.

1.note: Supported SB12 Parents' Bill of Rights (2025).

0.note: Campaign Issues: 'every town is a border town,' advocates state-led border operations / Operation Lone Star posture.

profile.religion: Christian (elder and Sunday-school teacher, Lifehouse Church, San Antonio)
profile.background: Northwest Bexar Republican, HD-122, in office since 2023 (succeeded moderate Lyle Larson). Construction-business owner, former Bexar GOP chair; Freedom Caucus-aligned. Won the March 2026 GOP primary, faces a Democrat in November 2026. Committees: Homeland Security/Public Safety & Veterans' Affairs, Human Services.

mark-dorazio@TX.notes_set: Evidence pass 2026-06-19. Consistently conservative across all areas; NO cross-party deviation. Texas Right to Life '100% Pro-Life'; YCT 88th-session score 94; Texas Scorecard Fiscal Index 77 (2023) -> 84 (2025). Cosponsor SB14 (youth gender ban), joint-sponsor SB2 vouchers, cosponsor SB10 (Ten Commandments)/SB965 (religious speech), SB33 (abortion funding), SB1362 (red-flag prohibition), SB1596 (NFA short-barrel), SB17 (foreign property restrictions); authored HB2306 (no parole for child-trafficking) and HB3053 (ban municipal gun buybacks). Was the only Bexar member to vote against impeaching AG Paxton (cited due-process). Weakest metric is a mid-range FRI, not a deviation.

0.note: Texas Right to Life rates him '100% Pro-Life'; endorsed by Texas Right to Life PAC and Texas Alliance for Life.

1.note: 100% pro-life voting record in post-Dobbs Texas (total-ban regime); cosponsor SB33 (bars taxpayer/logistical funding of out-of-state abortions) + medication-abortion restrictions.

2.note: Cosponsor of SB14 (2023), the ban on youth gender-transition procedures/hormones.

0.note: Voted to keep ESA/vouchers in the 2023 special session (one of two Bexar members) and joint-sponsored SB2 (2025) school-choice law.

3.note: Cosponsor SB10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms) and SB965 (religious-speech/prayer protections for school employees) (2025).

2.note: Authored HB2306 removing parole eligibility for child-trafficking offenses — proportional punishment of evildoers.

0.note: #1 campaign issue 'Securing Our Border'; supports $3.5B border security and Operation Lone Star.

2.note: Supports mandatory ICE cooperation / opposes sanctuary policies.

4.note: Cosponsor SB17 restricting foreign-entity ownership of Texas property/farmland.

1.note: Cosponsor SB1362 prohibiting red-flag (extreme-risk protective) orders.

2.note: Cosponsor SB1596 legalizing short-barrel firearms (NFA rollback at the state level).

4.note: Authored HB3053 banning municipal gun-buyback programs — anti-citizen-disarmament.

profile.background: San Antonio Democrat, HD-123 (Bexar Co.), in office since 2015; former SA City Council member and MALDEF civil-rights attorney (school-finance, voting rights). Former Vice Chair of House Public Education. Running unopposed in 2026 (primary and general).

diego-bernal@TX.notes_set: Evidence pass 2026-06-19. Consistent progressive Democrat; ZERO cross-party deviations. Texas Alliance for Life 2025 = 46%; voted NO on SB2 vouchers (2025, anti-voucher record, Texas Parent PAC endorsed); NO on SB763 school chaplains (2023); NO on HB1927 permitless carry (2021, tried to amend it) and supports gun restrictions (filed SB220). Participated in the July 2021 Democratic quorum break to D.C. to block the SB1 election bill. Religion undocumented. SB14/SB4/SB10 individual votes not isolated in sources — left null rather than inferred from party.

1.note: Texas Alliance for Life 2025 rated him 46%; voted against an abortion-restriction measure on the House floor (May 2025).

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 (2025) vouchers; long anti-voucher record as Public Education vice chair; endorsed by Texas Parent PAC.

1.note: Joined the July 2021 Democratic quorum break to D.C. to block SB1, which contained voter-ID/citizenship and mail-in provisions.

3.note: Same quorum break opposed SB1's restrictions on mass mail-in voting and ballot delivery.

0.note: Voted NO on HB1927 (2021 permitless carry) and co-filed an amendment to restrict license-free carry.

1.note: Supports gun restrictions; filed SB220 barring open carry in mental-health facilities.
